title: Topology Declarations, Policies, and Migration · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a versioned Go topology ownership manifest with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a versioned Go topology ownership manifest and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Make QueueDeclare and ExchangeDeclare calls equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and classify precondition errors.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
Order the Topology Migration RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Topology Migration assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration, keep Topology Migration connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go messaging team handles case 9984 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Topology Migration has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Topology Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 9984 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Topology Migration, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Redeploying all services simultaneously makes destructive queue changes atomic. In Go, retrying a QueueDeclare precondition error cannot mutate the existing queue.

options.text: Declare RabbitMQ and Go outcome, topology, ownership, identity, and byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets for the debug Topology Migration case
Predict RabbitMQ and Go publish, route, custody, delivery, effect, failure, cancellation, and recovery outcomes for the debug Topology Migration case
Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Topology Migration case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the debug Topology Migration case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-topology-migration-xbbk0c" that uses case 8013 to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Migration.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Topology Migration structural check is only one gate. a versioned Go topology ownership manifest must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Migration, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Topology Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Migration, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 6555 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; reject stale connection and channel generations; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Topology Migration, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Redeploying all services simultaneously makes destructive queue changes atomic. In Go, retrying a QueueDeclare precondition error cannot mutate the existing queue.
